title: श्री बुध सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री बुध सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥

प्रियङ्गुकल्पद्रुमतुल्यगात्रं
श्रेष्ठं पीताम्बरधरं चतुर्भुजम्।
खड्गं च चर्म गदया सुपुस्तकं
बुधं नमामि शशिजं किरीटिनम्॥

हरितवर्णं सुमुखं सौम्यं
सिंहवाहनमारूढम्।
पीतपुष्पप्रियं देवं
बुधं ध्यायेत् सदा बुधः॥

॥ अथ बुध सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

बुधो बुद्धिप्रदश्चैव बुद्धिमान् बुद्धिवर्धनः।
विद्यादो वाक्प्रदश्चैव वाणीनाथो गुणाकरः॥ १॥

सोमपुत्रः सोमवंश्यो रोहिणीसुत ईश्वरः।
सौम्यो हरिद्वर्णदेहः पीताम्बरधरो ग्रहः॥ २॥

कन्याराशीशो मिथुनेशो व्यापारकारकः।
वाणिज्यपतिर्गणितज्ञो ज्योतिषां पतिरेव च॥ ३॥

तारकासुतसन्दिग्धो ब्रह्मविद्याविशारदः।
चतुर्वेदविदां श्रेष्ठो षड्दर्शनविचक्षणः॥ ४॥

वाचस्पतिसमो बुद्ध्या कवित्वशक्तिदायकः।
लेखनकलाकुशलो गणनापटुरेव च॥ ५॥

सर्वविद्याधिदेवश्च सर्वशास्त्रविशारदः।
तर्कशास्त्रविदां नाथो न्यायशास्त्रपरायणः॥ ६॥

वैश्यवर्णाधिपो मान्यो वाणिज्यधनदायकः।
विज्ञानप्रदमार्गज्ञो गणकश्रेष्ठ एव च॥ ७॥

हरिप्रियो विष्णुभक्तो नारायणपरायणः।
उत्तरदिग्गतो देवो उत्तरायणवल्लभः॥ ८॥

धनदो विद्यादो बुद्ध्या वाक्सिद्धिकारकः।
सन्तानदो मित्रकारो बन्धुवर्गसुखप्रदः॥ ९॥

त्रिदोषनाशकश्चैव त्वग्रोगनिवारकः।
वातपित्तकफहारी स्नायुरोगविनाशनः॥ १०॥

पन्नगमणिभूषाढ्यो हरिद्रापुष्पवल्लभः।
मुद्गदालप्रियो देवो बुधवारप्रवर्तकः॥ ११॥

विद्यार्थिवत्सलो मान्यो वक्तृत्वबलदायकः।
स्मृतिवर्धन एव च परीक्षासिद्धिदायकः॥ १२॥

सर्वकलासु निपुणो यन्त्रतन्त्रविशारदः।
ज्ञानयोगप्रदो धीरो सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायकः॥ १३॥

व्यवसायफलदश्च मातुलतुल्यवत्सलः।
नपुंसकग्रहो बालो अल्पायुश्चिरायुषाम्॥ १४॥

मन्त्रसिद्धिप्रदो देवो यन्त्रसिद्धिकरो ग्रहः।
तन्त्रसाधनकर्ता च सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायकः॥ १५॥

युवराजो राजमान्यो राजसभासमालकः।
मन्त्रिपदप्रदश्चैव राजनीतिविशारदः॥ १६॥

लेखकश्रेष्ठदश्चैव भाषाविदां गुरुस्तथा।
व्याकरणज्ञो छन्दोविद् अलङ्कारविदेव च॥ १७॥

सङ्गीतकलाविद्वांश्च नाट्यशास्त्रविशारदः।
चित्रकलासु निपुणश्चित्रकारप्रवर्तकः॥ १८॥

धनुर्विद्यासु कुशलो अश्वविद्यासु पण्डितः।
आयुर्वेदविदां श्रेष्ठो चिकित्साशास्त्रवित्तमः॥ १९॥

सर्वज्ञः सर्वदृष्टिश्च सर्वकर्मफलप्रदः।
सर्ववेदान्तवेद्यश्च सर्वशास्त्रार्थतत्त्ववित्॥ २०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥

बुधसहस्रनाम्नां तु पठनाद्यत्फलं लभेत्।
विद्यां बुद्धिं धनं कीर्तिं वाक्सिद्धिं चाप्नुयान्नरः॥

बुधवारे तु यः पठेन्नियमेन समाहितः।
बुधदोषविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वविद्यामवाप्नुयात्॥

परीक्षासु जयं प्राप्य व्यापारे लाभमुत्तमम्।
बुधनामसहस्रस्य पठनात्सर्वमाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री स्कन्दपुराणोक्तं श्री बुध सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Budh Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||

Priyangu-kalpa-druma-tulya-gatram
Shreshtham Pitambara-dharam Chatur-bhujam |
Khadgam cha Charma Gadaya Su-pustakam
Budham namami Shashi-jam Kiritinam ||

Harita-varnam Su-mukham Saumyam
Simha-vahanam-arudham |
Pita-pushpa-priyam Devam
Budham dhyayet sada Budhah ||

|| Atha Budh Sahasranama Stotram ||

Budho Buddhi-pradash-chaiva Buddhiman Buddhi-vardhanah |
Vidya-do Vak-pradash-chaiva Vani-natho Guna-karah || 1 ||

Soma-putrah Soma-vamshyo Rohini-suta Ishwarah |
Saumyo Harid-varna-dehah Pitambara-dharo Grahah || 2 ||

Kanya-rashisho Mithuneso Vyapara-karakah |
Vanijya-patir-Ganita-jno Jyotisham patir-eva cha || 3 ||

Taraka-suta-sandigdho Brahma-vidya-visharadah |
Chatur-veda-vidam shreshtho Shad-darshana-vichakshanah || 4 ||

Vachaspati-samo buddhya Kavitva-shakti-dayakah |
Lekhana-kala-kushalo Ganana-patur-eva cha || 5 ||

Sarva-vidya-adhi-devash-cha Sarva-shastra-visharadah |
Tarka-shastra-vidam natho Nyaya-shastra-parayanah || 6 ||

Vaishya-varna-adhipo Manyo Vanijya-dhana-dayakah |
Vijnana-prada-marga-jno Ganaka-shreshtha eva cha || 7 ||

Hari-priyo Vishnu-bhakto Narayana-parayanah |
Uttara-dig-gato Devo Uttarayana-vallabhah || 8 ||

Dhana-do Vidya-do Buddhya Vak-siddhi-karakah |
Santana-do Mitra-karo Bandhu-varga-sukha-pradah || 9 ||

Tri-dosha-nashakash-chaiva Tvag-roga-nivarakah |
Vata-pitta-kapha-hari Snayu-roga-vinashanah || 10 ||

Pannaga-mani-bhushadhyo Haridra-pushpa-vallabhah |
Mudga-dala-priyo Devo Budhavara-pravartakah || 11 ||

Vidyarthi-vatsalo Manyo Vaktritva-bala-dayakah |
Smriti-vardhana eva cha Pariksha-siddhi-dayakah || 12 ||

Sarva-kalasu nipuno Yantra-tantra-visharadah |
Jnana-yoga-prado Dhiro Sarva-sampat-pradayakah || 13 ||

Vyavasaya-phala-dash-cha Matula-tulya-vatsalah |
Napumsaka-graho Balo Alpa-ayush-chirayusham || 14 ||

Mantra-siddhi-prado Devo Yantra-siddhi-karo Grahah |
Tantra-sadhana-karta cha Sarva-siddhi-pradayakah || 15 ||

Yuvarajo Raja-manyo Raja-sabha-samalakah |
Mantri-pada-pradash-chaiva Rajaniti-visharadah || 16 ||

Lekhaka-shreshtha-dash-chaiva Bhasha-vidam Gurus-tatha |
Vyakarana-jno Chhando-vid Alankara-vid-eva cha || 17 ||

Sangita-kala-vidvamsh-cha Natya-shastra-visharadah |
Chitra-kalasu nipunash-Chitra-kara-pravartakah || 18 ||

Dhanur-vidyasu kushalo Ashva-vidyasu panditah |
Ayurveda-vidam shreshtho Chikitsa-shastra-vittamah || 19 ||

Sarvajnah Sarva-drishtish-cha Sarva-karma-phala-pradah |
Sarva-vedanta-vedyash-cha Sarva-shastra-artha-tattva-vit || 20 ||

|| Phalashruti ||

Budha-Sahasra-namnam tu pathanad-yat-phalam labhet |
Vidyam Buddhim Dhanam Kirtim Vak-siddhim cha-apnuyan-narah ||

Budhavare tu yah pathen-niyamena samahitah |
Budha-dosha-vinirmuktah Sarva-vidyam-avapnuyat ||

Pariksasu jayam prapya vyapare labham-uttamam |
Budha-nama-sahasrasya pathanat-sarvam-apnuyat ||

|| Iti Shri Skanda-Purana-uktam Shri Budh Sahasranama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: प्रियंगुकल्पद्रुम तुल्य गात्र, पीताम्बर, चतुर्भुज, खड्ग-चर्म-गदा-पुस्तक धारी, किरीटी, शशिज बुध का ध्यान करें। बुध, बुद्धिप्रद, बुद्धिमान, बुद्धिवर्धक, विद्यादायक, वाक्प्रद, सोमपुत्र, सौम्य, कन्या-मिथुन राशीश, व्यापारकारक, वाणिज्यपति, गणितज्ञ — ये बुध के प्रमुख नाम हैं। ब्रह्मविद्या विशारद, चतुर्वेदविदों में श्रेष्ठ, षड्दर्शन विचक्षण, वाचस्पति समो बुद्ध्या, कवित्वशक्ति दायक बुध। बुधवार को पठन से बुधदोष निवृत्ति, विद्या, बुद्धि, धन, कीर्ति, वाक्सिद्धि, परीक्षाजय और व्यापारलाभ प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Invokes intelligence, wisdom, and academic success
Traditionally recited for removal of budh dosha from horoscope
Invokes the blessing of eloquence and mastery of speech
Invokes success in examinations and competitive tests
Traditionally associated with profit in business and trade
Invokes the blessing of knowledge of all arts and sciences
description: Budh Sahasranama Stotram — श्री बुध सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् — is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names offered to the Nine Planets (Navagraha). Drawn from Skanda Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to bestow knowledge of all arts and sciences, remove Budh dosha from horoscope, and grant success in examinations and competitive tests. This sacred text is ideally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) for enhanced blessings. At 23 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 25 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Budh Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Budh Sahasranama in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Mercury planet for intelligence, education and business success.

Budh weak chart remedy stotram chanting

seoFaqs.question: What is the canonical source of the Budh Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Budh Sahasranama is preserved within the Skanda Purana and the Padma Purana, both of which contain extended Navagraha-stotra sections dedicated to each of the nine planetary deities. The Padma Purana version is the more widely circulated and forms the basis of most published editions. Within the broader Navagraha sahasranama corpus, each graha (Surya, Chandra, Mangala, Budha, Brihaspati, Shukra, Shani, Rahu, Ketu) has a thousand-name stotra structured along similar lines, opening with the bija mantra of the planet, proceeding through one thousand epithets that describe the deity's attributes, planetary qualities and cosmic role, and closing with a phalashruti enumerating the benefits of recitation. The Budh version is specifically recited for Mercury-related afflictions in the natal chart. (Sources: Wikipedia Navagraha; drikpanchang.com Lord Budha; tirumalatirupatiyatra.in; astrosage.com Budha.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Budh in Hindu mythology and how does this background inform the Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Budh is the deva of the planet Mercury, born of the union of Chandra (the Moon) and Tara, the wife of Brihaspati (Jupiter), in a famous Puranic episode in which Chandra abducted Tara. Budh inherits attributes from both his celestial father (the lustrous beauty and changeability of the Moon) and his stepfather (the wisdom and learning of Jupiter). He is described as gentle, complex in nature, often androgynous in iconography, and is the lord of buddhi (intellect), vak (speech), commerce, mathematics and craft. The Sahasranama therefore weaves together attributes from both lineages: epithets of cool moon-like beauty, of crisp Jovian intellect, and of the planetary domains of trade, communication and learning. (Sources: Wikipedia Navagraha; drikpanchang.com Lord Budha; tirumalatirupatiyatra.in; askganesha.com Budh mantra.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Budh Sahasranama most powerfully recited in the weekly and natal-chart cycle?
seoFaqs.answer: Wednesday (Budhwar) is Budh's day of the week and the principal recitation day. Within the day, the Mercury-hora (the planetary hour ruled by Mercury, calculable from sunrise) is the most concentrated window. Annually, the day Mercury enters the rashi of the devotee's natal Moon-sign and the day of Mercury's stationary direct (after retrograde) are powerful occasions. For natal-chart remediation, the Sahasranama is recited as part of a Budha shanti puja prescribed when Mercury is debilitated, combust, retrograde, in the dusthana houses (6, 8, 12), or in mahadasha-antardasha periods of Mercury that show malefic results. A 41-day or 108-day parayana is the typical remedy length. (Sources: shreemaa.org Budha; astrosage.com; vedicrishi.in Mercury; mahakatha.com Budh mantra.)

seoFaqs.question: What specific benefits does the Sahasranama claim for daily recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The Budh Sahasranama phalashruti emphasises: enhancement of buddhi (analytic intelligence and discriminating judgment); development of fluent and persuasive speech; success in trade, commerce and business negotiations; mastery of writing, language and mathematics; victory in legal disputes and contract negotiations (Mercury rules contracts); good health of the nervous system and skin (Budha's karaka domains); harmonious relationships with maternal uncles, cousins and siblings (Mercury's karaka-relations); and recovery from speech impediments, dyslexia and learning difficulties in children. Students preparing for examinations, lawyers, traders, journalists and writers traditionally undertake Budh-recitation; parents recite on behalf of children with educational struggles. (Sources: askganesha.com Budh mantra; mahakatha.com; vedicrishi.in Mercury; shreemaa.org.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Budh Sahasranama relate to Vishnu Sahasranama, traditionally prescribed for Mercury remediation?
seoFaqs.answer: Vishnu is the presiding deity (adhi-devata) of Mercury in the Navagraha-adhi-devata system, with the prati-devata being Narayana. As a result, classical jyotisha texts including the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra prescribe the recitation of Vishnu Sahasranama as the most powerful remedy for any Mercury-related affliction, sometimes more emphatically than the Budh Sahasranama itself. A serious sadhaka working on Mercury issues typically recites both: Vishnu Sahasranama for the underlying adhi-devata blessing and Budh Sahasranama for direct planetary appeasement. The Vishnu Sahasranama is also chanted on Wednesdays for this reason in many South Indian households, even by devotees who do not regularly engage with planetary worship. (Sources: hindupedia.com Vishnu Sahasranamam; hinduismfacts.org; templepurohit.com Vishnu Sahasranamam; astrosage.com Budha.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Budh Sahasranama compare with the shorter Budh Pancha Vimshati Nama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Budh Pancha Vimshati Nama Stotram is a 25-name version of the Mercury stotra, suited to daily householder recitation in 5 to 7 minutes. The Sahasranama, with its 1000 names, takes 30 to 45 minutes for a careful recitation and is undertaken either on Wednesdays as a longer weekly observance or as a parayana during Mercury-remediation. The 25 names of the Pancha Vimshati are essentially a curated essence of the Sahasranama, including the most important epithets like Budh, Saumya, Shyama, Tara-suta (son of Tara), Soma-putra (son of Soma), Jna (the knower), Bodha (awakening), and Shubha-prada (bestower of auspiciousness). Devotees who cannot commit to daily Sahasranama recitation use the Pancha Vimshati for daily practice and the Sahasranama on Wednesdays only. (Sources: hindupedia.com Budha Pancha Vimsathi; templesinindiainfo.com 108 Names Budha; shreemaa.org Budha.)
